goal: Enforce consistent visual styling across all Aether commands — caste emojis, spaced-letter banners, progress bars, and ant spawn announcements

phases.name: Banner standardization
phases.description: Replace prohibited dash banners with heavy-rule banners in init.md (Claude + OpenCode)

success_criteria: Zero dash banners in either init.md file

phases.name: Spawn announcement conformance
phases.description: Fix all non-conforming spawn announcements to use the canonical two-line pattern across all commands and playbooks

tasks.description: Fix seal.md: Sage spawn to two-line pattern, remove Chronicler redundant third line
Fix build-verify.md: add task description to Watcher spawn, remove Measurer redundant third line
Fix build-wave.md: remove Ambassador redundant third line

Apply matching spawn fixes to OpenCode build.md and continue.md mirrors

phases.name: Emoji map and progress bars
phases.description: Add missing emoji entries and integrate progress bars into build phase start

success_criteria: generate-progress-bar called in build-prep.md and OpenCode build.md

phases.name: Verification sweep
phases.description: Run comprehensive visual audit and full test suite to confirm all fixes

tasks.description: Grep audit: verify zero dash banners, zero orphaned spawns, emoji map count is 28
Run npm test to verify zero regressions

success_criteria: npm test passes with zero failures

phases.name: Stabilize caste emojis in spawn and phase displays
phases.description: Add caste emojis to all ant spawn announcements and phase header displays across all commands — every spawn shows its caste emoji and phase headers include visual emoji markers

tasks.description: Diagnose and correct: Add caste emojis to all ant spawn announcements and phase header displays across all commands — every spawn shows its caste emoji and phase headers include visual emoji markers

success_criteria: No regressions introduced in adjacent flows
Inserted-phase objective is resolved in real usage
User confirms expected behavior after changes

learnings.claim: init.md was the only command using prohibited dash banners — all other commands already used heavy-rule correctly
